dimensions.name: coverage
dimensions.evidence: `definition.yaml` defines 6 deliverables and `plan.json` assigns each exactly once across waves 1-6: context-construction-patterns, handoff-schema, context-routing-cli, layer-architecture, boundary-enforcement, artifact-presentation-protocol.

dimensions.name: feasibility
dimensions.evidence: Wave ordering respects all declared dependencies (`context-routing-cli` after `context-construction-patterns`, `layer-architecture` after `handoff-schema` and `context-routing-cli`, `boundary-enforcement` after `layer-architecture`, `artifact-presentation-protocol` last), and each wave contains only one deliverable so there are no intra-wave file-ownership overlaps.

dimensions.name: specificity
dimensions.evidence: Every `plan.json` wave assignment includes a concrete `specialist` and a non-empty `file_ownership` list, with ownership scoped to explicit paths rather than vague areas.

dimensions.name: research-grounding
dimensions.evidence: The decisions are justified by T1/T2/T3 findings in `summary.md`, but they do not consistently cite decision-specific research by exact file path and section as required; references are mostly topic-level or source-level rather than section-anchored citations.
